--- SOURCE 1: Original Article Dynamic response of ballasted High-Speed Railways ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2214391225000686

SUMMARY:
This study explored the dynamic behavior of ballasted railway tracks during high-speed train passages through field measurements and advanced numerical modeling

--- SOURCE 3: Modelling dynamic structures of fares in high-speed rail market ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352146525005952

SUMMARY:
The aim of the study is to identify and compare the characteristics of the fares evolution over time, by means of a method that allows a dynamic analysis.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 4: Numerical Modeling Techniques of High-Speed Rail Bridge Structures ---
URL: https://rosap.ntl.bts.gov/view/dot/72662

SUMMARY:
The focus of this research is to identify these modeling features and inherent characteristics of HSR bridges, and to provide guidance and demonstration

--- SOURCE 5: High Speed Railway Track Dynamics - SpringerLink ---
URL: https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-981-16-4593-8

SUMMARY:
This book summarizes the latest research findings on high-speed railway track dynamics, made by the author and his research team over the past decade.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Utilizing sophisticated numerical models to replicate dynamic responses in high-speed rail systems enhances predictive accuracy, as demonstrated in various studies on track dynamics.
Implementing continuous monitoring frameworks allows for real-time data collection on track performance, facilitating proactive maintenance and operational safety in high-speed rail networks.

Exploring innovative materials with superior durability and dynamic characteristics can significantly improve track performance and longevity, aligning with recent advancements in engineering technology.
